channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A big win for Trump's deportation agenda, with another fight looming
item.description: The Senate passed a major immigration enforcement funding bill. Now, the measure goes to the House. Lawmakers must also decide the future of a surveillance program set to expire next week.
item.content:encoded: The Senate passed a major immigration enforcement funding bill. Now, the measure goes to the House. Lawmakers must also decide the future of a surveillance program set to expire next week.
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: The return-to-work Survival Guide for new parents
item.description: Heading back to work after parental leave? Life Kit has some advice on navigating schedules, managing expectations and finding support during a major life transition.
item.content:encoded: Heading back to work after parental leave? Life Kit has some advice on navigating schedules, managing expectations and finding support during a major life transition.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: Trump is remaking U.S. policy in Latin America
item.description: Military strikes, sanctions and political pressure are becoming hallmarks of Trump's Latin America policy. NPR's Adrian Florido speaks with historian Greg Grandin about what's driving the shift.
item.content:encoded: Military strikes, sanctions and political pressure are becoming hallmarks of Trump's Latin America policy. NPR's Adrian Florido speaks with historian Greg Grandin about what's driving the shift.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: 'Race Unwrapped' podcast explores art as protest
item.description: The new season of the 'Race Unwrapped' podcast looks at art as a force for social change. Host Michelle Tyrene Johnson talks to NPR's Adrian Florido about the intersection of storytelling and protest.
item.content:encoded: The new season of the 'Race Unwrapped' podcast looks at art as a force for social change. Host Michelle Tyrene Johnson talks to NPR's Adrian Florido about the intersection of storytelling and protest.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Cineplexity: movies where the city of LA is a character, from Chinatown to Clueless
item.description: Los Angeles can be glamorous, gritty, sprawling and strange. NPR cinephiles discuss the movies that bring the city to life.
item.content:encoded: Los Angeles can be glamorous, gritty, sprawling and strange. NPR cinephiles discuss the movies that bring the city to life.
item.dc:creator: Marc Rivers

item.title: Pakistani workers say their faith cost them their jobs in the UAE
item.description: Shia Muslims from Pakistan say they're being deported from the UAE shortly after the Iran war began.
item.content:encoded: Shia Muslims from Pakistan say they're being deported from the UAE shortly after the Iran war began.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Can birds outsing traffic? Some are trying
item.description: New research from scientists at the Centre for Ecological Research in Hungary finds that some birds living in cities are changing their songs to compete with traffic and other urban noise.
item.content:encoded: New research from scientists at the Centre for Ecological Research in Hungary finds that some birds living in cities are changing their songs to compete with traffic and other urban noise.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Bulldozers in paradise: how a massive infrastructure project threatens an isolated island community
item.description: India plans to build a major port, airport and town on pristine, remote Great Nicobar Island. Supporters say it could boost trade and jobs, but critics worry about environmental damage.
item.content:encoded: India plans to build a major port, airport and town on pristine, remote Great Nicobar Island. Supporters say it could boost trade and jobs, but critics worry about environmental damage.
item.dc:creator: Omkar Khandekar

item.title: Can conservative Christian churches win back young women?
item.description: Conservative Christian leaders say winning over young women is a priority. Kathryn Post of Religion News Service explains how groups like Turning Point USA are responding.
item.content:encoded: Conservative Christian leaders say winning over young women is a priority. Kathryn Post of Religion News Service explains how groups like Turning Point USA are responding.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Officials are scrambling to understand the scope of the Ebola outbreak
item.description: Ebola cases are rising in Congo and Uganda. NPR's Jonathan Lambert explains why the outbreak may be even larger than official numbers show.
item.content:encoded: Ebola cases are rising in Congo and Uganda. NPR's Jonathan Lambert explains why the outbreak may be even larger than official numbers show.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert
